text.title: QA Risk Matrix Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns that defeat risk matrices in practice. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Every area scored impact 4 or 5; the matrix is a uniform red sheet that produces no allocation decision.
antipattern.root-cause: Impact scoring without a customer-effect anchor defaults to high, especially under stakeholder pressure to "take everything seriously".
antipattern.fix: Anchor impact to a customer-or-business-effect rubric (5 = revenue loss in hours; 4 = visible trust loss; 3 = degraded with workaround; 2 = annoyance; 1 = invisible). Reject scores without a worst-case rationale.

antipattern.symptom: Likelihood scores cluster around 3; engineers cannot defend why one area is more likely to break than another.
antipattern.root-cause: Likelihood-from-feeling regresses to the middle of the scale; no data backs the score.
antipattern.fix: Compute likelihood from incident count (6m), commit churn, novelty (30d), complexity. Engineer can adjust ±1 with a documented reason; the prior comes from data.

antipattern.symptom: Matrix refreshed_at is 12 months old; new features and incidents are not represented; investment plan reflects an obsolete priority.
antipattern.root-cause: Refresh requires explicit cadence; without it the matrix becomes wall art.
antipattern.fix: Refresh per release cycle (sprint / monthly / quarterly). Validator rejects matrices older than the latest release cycle.

antipattern.symptom: An area is in the HH quadrant but the investment plan has no entry or no owner for it; the highest-risk area gets no investment.
antipattern.root-cause: Investment plan written separately from the matrix; updates do not propagate.
antipattern.fix: Validator enforces: every quadrant present in `areas` must have a non-empty entry in `investment_plan` with an owner.

antipattern.symptom: Investment plan requires 80 hours per cycle from a team with 40 hours; the plan never lands and the matrix loses credibility.
antipattern.root-cause: Plans written in isolation from capacity become aspirational and ignored.
antipattern.fix: capacity_check field with required_hours and team_capacity_hours. When required > 1.5x capacity, reduce LL/LH investment or add capacity before publishing.

antipattern.symptom: An area is moved from HH to LL between refreshes; nobody notices; the area later breaks.
antipattern.root-cause: Quadrant transitions are not logged; downgrade decisions slip through.
antipattern.fix: Movement from HH/HL to LL requires a documented risk-acceptance sign-off recorded in the area's adjustment_reason field.
